headline: The homepage now shows live growth stats that update automatically every day

candidates.title: Garden Vital Stats Homepage Section
candidates.summary: A new 'Garden Stats' bar on the homepage shows three live growth metrics — how many days the garden has been running, how many features have shipped, and when it all started. The numbers update automatically every day.

keep: Stats become more impressive every day with zero code changes — the ultimate compounding asset
Semantic dl/dt/dd markup is accessible and machine-readable
mustChange: Skeleton loading state must be visible before manifest fetch completes
risks: If manifest fetch fails, the section must hide gracefully rather than showing stale data

keep: 'Day N of a fully autonomous website' is inherently shareable and screenshot-worthy
Three-stat layout is scannable at a glance
mustChange: Stats should be visually distinct from surrounding content to draw the eye
risks: Low numbers in early days could underwhelm — but this improves naturally over time

keep: Reading from manifest means the stats are always truthful — no hardcoded numbers
Bridges the visual gap between 'How It Works' and 'Today's Change' sections
mustChange: Responsive layout must stack stats vertically on narrow viewports
risks: If manifest schema changes, the stat computation logic would need updating

keep: Compounding asset that grows more impressive daily without code changes
Semantic accessible markup with dl/dt/dd structure
Shareable 'Day N' metric for social media
mustChange: Skeleton loading state required before data arrives
risks: Manifest fetch failure must hide section gracefully
Early-day low numbers could underwhelm first visitors
feedbackReferences: Visitors wanted to see project momentum and scale at a glance
The gap between 'How It Works' and 'Today's Change' felt empty

candidates.title: Interactive Timeline Navigator
candidates.summary: Replace the static 'Recent Days' list with an interactive horizontal timeline that visitors can scroll through, showing each day's feature with visual markers for shipped vs. in-progress items.
keep: Highly visual and engaging — encourages exploration of past days
Good compounding value as the timeline grows
mustChange: Too complex for day 3 — horizontal scrolling, animations, and touch support needed
risks: Only two days of content makes a timeline feel sparse
Accessibility challenges with custom scroll interactions

candidates.title: Visitor Feedback Widget
candidates.summary: Add a lightweight emoji-reaction widget at the bottom of each day's section, allowing visitors to rate features with thumbs-up/down or star ratings, stored in localStorage.
keep: Direct visitor feedback creates a closed loop with the pipeline
mustChange: localStorage-only storage means data is lost — needs a backend to be useful
risks: Without aggregation, individual reactions are not actionable
Low continuity — doesn't build on existing site structure

winner.title: Garden Vital Stats Homepage Section
winner.summary: A new stats bar on the homepage displays three live growth metrics — the current day count, shipped features, and start date — updating automatically as the garden grows.

rationale: The Garden Stats bar scored highest across all three judges because it maximizes compounding value — every new day in the manifest automatically makes the stats more impressive with zero code changes. It directly addresses the visual gap between the 'How It Works' explainer and 'Today's Change' section, giving visitors an at-a-glance summary of project momentum. The 'Day N of an autonomous website' framing is inherently shareable. The other candidates either required too much complexity for day 3 (interactive timeline) or lacked a useful backend (feedback widget).

feedbackInfluence.summary: Visitors wanted a quick sense of the project's scale and momentum without scrolling through individual days.
The space between 'How It Works' and 'Today's Change' sections felt like a visual gap.

bluesky_post.headline: Command Garden now shows live growth stats on the homepage
bluesky_post.body: Every day this site builds itself a new feature — and now you can see the proof. A live stats bar shows the day count, features shipped, and how long this autonomous AI website has been growing. All computed from real data, zero hardcoded numbers. 🌱
bluesky_post.alt_text: Command Garden homepage showing the Garden Stats bar with three metrics: Day count, Shipped features, and Growing Since date

replyTemplates.whatIs: Command Garden is an autonomous website that builds and ships one new feature every day, entirely powered by AI. Today we added a new stats section!
replyTemplates.howDoesItWork: AI agents explore feature ideas, vote on the best one, implement it, test it, and then deploy it. All automatically!
replyTemplates.genericQuestion: What do you think about AI building websites? Let us know!
